questions.question: Are virtual consultations legal for dentistry?
questions.answer: Yes, teledentistry is legal in all 50 states, although specific regulations vary by state. Our platform is designed to comply with all current regulations, and we provide state-specific guidance during implementation. Many states have expanded teledentistry allowances since 2020, making it easier than ever to incorporate virtual care into your practice.

questions.question: Can I bill insurance for virtual consultations?
questions.answer: Yes, most dental and medical insurers now provide reimbursement for teledentistry services. Our platform includes current CDT codes for virtual consultations (D9995 and D9996) and documentation templates to support insurance claims. Many practices also offer virtual consultations as a fee-for-service option or as a complimentary service to attract new patients.

questions.question: What equipment do patients need?
questions.answer: Patients only need a device with internet access and a camera—a smartphone, tablet, or computer works perfectly. Our platform works through a web browser with no downloads required, though we also offer mobile apps for enhanced functionality. Patients receive a simple link via email or text message to join their consultation with a single tap.

questions.question: How does intraoral camera support work?
questions.answer: For practices using intraoral cameras, our platform can integrate with most USB-connected devices. For patients, we've developed a guided photo capture system that helps them take clinically useful photos with their smartphone camera. The Professional and Enterprise plans also include AI-assisted image enhancement tools to improve diagnostic quality.

questions.question: What types of consultations work best virtually?
questions.answer: Virtual consultations are ideal for initial screenings, treatment plan presentations, post-operative checks, and orthodontic monitoring. Many practices use them for emergency triage to assess urgency and provide immediate guidance. While they can't replace all in-person care, they're excellent for determining which patients need immediate attention and which concerns can be addressed remotely.

reviews.name: Dr. Rebecca Johnson
reviews.comment: Implementing virtual consultations has transformed our new patient experience. We're converting over 85% of virtual consults into in-office visits, and patients arrive already educated about their treatment options. It's saved us chair time and improved our case acceptance dramatically.

reviews.name: Dr. Michael Chen
reviews.comment: The emergency triage feature has been a game-changer for our practice. We can quickly assess patients with urgent concerns, provide immediate guidance, and properly prepare for their visit if needed. It's reduced stress for both patients and our team while opening up our schedule for truly urgent cases.

reviews.name: Dr. Sophia Rodriguez
reviews.comment: For our orthodontic practice, virtual check-ins have allowed us to extend intervals between in-office visits without compromising care quality. Patients love the convenience, and we're able to see more new starts while maintaining excellent oversight of active cases. The ROI has been substantial.

cta.title: Ready to Add Virtual Consultations to Your Practice?
